speaker: Narrator
text: The player controls a blue sprite of Kris, which has just layered over a red SOUL. At first, the player is helpless, wandering the map in search of a sword and eventually stumbling upon a hatch. The sword is found. First, Kris destroys purple ghosts, reaching level two, then lime-green ones (which throw arrow projectiles) and another pair of purple ones, finally beating the only Fish on the map and leveling up to LV3. Now the player can chop down cacti. Then he enters an ice zone, where he fights enemies and reaches LV4. (The player can even destroy trees). After that, the player looks for a place on the map where there are trees instead of a wall and enters a pseudo-infinite forest. The player must chop down trees and move to 'Adjacent cells' until they find a red chest containing the Ice-key.

text: The TV screen turns on. After a series of glitches and title cards, the final session of the 8-bit game "Mantle" begins. A red heart appears on a black background, over which THE HERO is layered again.

context: Scene: Obj Mantle Game Sword Route Final
speaker: Narrator
text: Having reached LV3, the hero cuts the red ribbon and enters a dark dungeon. A long and tedious experience grind begins, where the hero fights numerous monsters to reach the maximum level.

speaker: Narrator
text: After defeating Tenna, the hero finds the exit from the dungeon. The game screen goes dark. When it turns on again, the pixelated hero delivers a blow that seems to knock the ODDCONTROLLER out of the real Kris's hands. The controller falls to the floor.

speaker: Narrator
text: Kris finds himself alone in a quiet, sunlit meadow. In front of him is the entrance to a Shelter.

speaker: Narrator
text: As you try to move Kris towards the shelter's entrance, his sprite actively resists. He repeatedly tries to turn and walk back, fighting against your control.

speaker: Narrator
text: After a brief struggle, you force Kris to enter the shelter.

speaker: Narrator
text: Inside, it is dark and quiet. Kris walks down a long corridor. At the end, he finds an intricate room.
Kris... oh... Kris... Is it fun, Kris? around like this...
That's why you're searching for them, aren't you?
The SHADOW CRYSTALs... ... and the SHADOW MANTLE that I'm holding!
Do you honestly think it'll get you what you want...?
... no, part of you is just... enjoying this, isn't it?
Oh, don't make that sour face. I can see in the dark, you know!
The question is... Can you?

speaker: Narrator
text: A non-turn-based, bullet-hell style battle begins with a bat-like creature. The battle has several phases, each more difficult than the last.

speaker: Narrator
text: [(Phase 1)] The enemy attacks with simple bombs.

speaker: Narrator
text: [(Phase 2)] The attacks become more intense, and the enemy now summons 'Image_friend'-like entities.

speaker: Narrator
text: [(Phase 3)] The enemy begins to spew six projectiles in different trajectories from itself.

speaker: Narrator
text: [(Phase 4)] The enemy summons flamethrower-like lines of fire.

speaker: Narrator
text: [(Final Phase)] The background turns red. The enemy uses all its attacks simultaneously. The hero delivers a devastating blow, defeating the enemy.

speaker: Narrator
text: After the victory, the screen darkens. Text from an unknown voice appears.
There! That's what I wanted to see!
Flickering red, like pretty little flames.
Your eyes can't hide it, Kris. Without play...
The knife grows dull.
Haha... well, enough of that! We both have work to do!
So if you want this MANTLE, hurry up and take it.
If you can reach it!

text: The silhouettes begin to disappear, one by one, in a precise, agonizing order. Dess disappears first, her figure just vanishes into thin air. Carol follows her. After them, Kris's brother leaves, his silhouette goes off into the distance, towards the college. Even Kris himself seems to be moving away, his figure is getting dimmer. In the end, only Toriel remains, but her attention dissipates over time, absorbed by worries.

text: Mr. Tenna... I... understand how you feel.

text: To want to be... important. To be... useful.

speaker: Ralsei
text: Perhaps... you might not be watched much anymore...

speaker: Ralsei
text: But... that doesn't make you a failure, Tenna.

speaker: Ralsei
text: You've brought smiles, light into Lightner's lives...

speaker: Ralsei
text: ... to Kris's family and friends, for so long.

speaker: Ralsei
text: So, there's nothing to be ashamed of.

speaker: Ralsei
text: If... that ever comes to an end.

speaker: Ralsei
text: Darkners... all become obsolete eventually.

speaker: Ralsei
text: But we aren't "real", Tenna.

speaker: Ralsei
text: We shouldn't make Lightners worry about what happens to us.

speaker: Ralsei
text: It'd just... make them unhappy, wouldn't it?

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: m... m... maybe... you're right...

speaker: Susie
text: Hey.

speaker: Susie
text: You wanna know what I think?

speaker: Ralsei
text: Susie?

speaker: Susie
text: I think...

speaker: Narrator
text: Susie approaches Ralsei and Kris.

speaker: Susie
text: You don't have to take being thrown away.

speaker: Narrator
text: The light focuses on Susie.

speaker: Susie
text: Lemme tell you.

speaker: Susie
text: I've never really been able to... make friends.

speaker: Susie
text: I was always... the scary girl. The bad kid.

speaker: Susie
text: The only times anyone ever got close to me,

speaker: Susie
text: ... were as a joke.

speaker: Susie
text: And even if I did start to make a real friend...

speaker: Susie
text: ... I'd always end up...

speaker: Susie
text: Moving away.

speaker: Narrator
text: Flashbacks appear: an empty classroom, a graveyard, a diner.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: When I moved here, I thought it would be the same thing.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: I felt like you.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: I felt like a broken toy no one wanted.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: Kris's mom saw me. Toriel.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: Saw me sitting on the bench in the graveyard, crying.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: She asked me what was wrong, and...

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: Told me everything was going to be okay.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: Took me to the diner. Bought me a hot chocolate.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: Talked to me. Told me I'd make friends.

speaker: Susie (voiceover)
text: That... gave me hope. Even just a little.

speaker: Narrator
text: The flashbacks end. The scene returns to the team.

speaker: Susie
text: And recently. That hope's been growing.

speaker: Susie
text: Because... now I have friends.

speaker: Susie
text: Real friends.

speaker: Susie
text: Friends I wanna keep.

speaker: Susie
text: Kris. Ralsei. Lancer... Toriel, too.

speaker: Susie
text: Even though I thought I was broken...

speaker: Susie
text: I just needed to find the right people, y'know?

speaker: Susie
text: So...

speaker: Susie
text: Don't worry, dude.

speaker: Susie
text: Someone out there wants you. Promise.

speaker: Susie
text: Kris, you'll help me ask around town, right?

speaker: Susie
text: Someone's gotta want a TV.

text: Mike (voice from the spotlight):** * You... you'll help me?

text: Mike (voice from the spotlight):** * SUSIE!!! You...

text: You're INCREDIBLE! You're AMAZING!! No... you're MARVELOUS!!

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: I feel electric!! I feel like a million bucks!!

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: This... this must be what HOPE feels like!!

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: We're inventing a Hall of Fame just for you!!!

speaker: Narrator
text: Susie turns to him.

speaker: Susie
text: But... you gotta give back Toriel.

speaker: Susie
text: She's got people that need her, too.

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: I understand!

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: I know it's not what the KNIGHT wants, but...

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: Kris!! Susie!! Good contestants deserve a good prize!!

speaker: Mr. Tenna
text: This way!!

speaker: Susie

speaker: Susie
text: Not you. Not me. Not Kris.

speaker: Susie
text: Got it?

speaker: Ralsei

speaker: Susie

speaker: Susie

text: It's some sort of... electronic... lock?

speaker: Susie
text: Kris, what the hell is going on...!?

text: Susie pounds on the door again.

text: What the hell are they doing, Kris? Like, if the cops...

speaker: Susie
text: This... weird panel. If we could just... get someone to tell us how it works...

speaker: Susie
text: We can't tell anyone about this, right? Because...

text: ... Because you saw that smoke, right, Kris?

text: There's... gotta be a dark world in there.

text: Kris is left alone in front of the bunker door. He approaches it.

text: On the shelter was a strange panel.

text: It looked like a digital security lock...
